description: Detects std::endl usage in C++ -- prefer '\n' to avoid unnecessary stream flushing. Simplified from Rust: flags all qualified_identifier nodes equal to std::endl or std::flush (info severity). NOLINT suppression, loop-context severity escalation (warning inside loops), and bare endl/flush after 'using namespace std' are not supported in the JSON DSL.

flag.message: std::endl/std::flush detected -- prefer '\n' to avoid unnecessary stream buffer flushing
